skill.name: content-agent
skill.description: Generates personalized marketing content for Unite-Hub. Creates followup emails, proposals, and case studies based on contact data and interaction history. Uses Claude AI for high-quality, contextual content generation.
skill.summary: Generates personalized B2B marketing emails, proposals, and case study references from contact context.
skill.author: CleanExpo

security_audit.summary: The static Ruby backtick findings are false positives caused by Markdown code fences, not executable commands. The weak cryptography and network reconnaissance flags also lack evidence in SKILL.md. The remaining risk is intentional network use: contact context is sent to the Anthropic API and generated prompts are stored, so publication should include a privacy warning.

medium_findings.title: External AI API Receives Contact Context
medium_findings.description: The skill documents sending a built prompt to the Anthropic messages API. Earlier lines build that prompt from names, companies, job titles, industry, last interaction, sentiment, and case study context, so contact and business data may leave the workspace.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network destination and prompt fields are explicitly documented. The finding is medium rather than high because this is the skill's stated function, not hidden exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Generated Prompts And Responses Are Persisted
low_findings.description: The skill stores the used prompt and generated response in a draft content record. This can retain personal contact details or sensitive sales context if prompts are not minimized or governed by retention controls.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The storage fields for prompt and text are shown directly. The exact sensitivity depends on the caller's input data and retention policy.

low_findings.title: Audit Log Includes Contact Metadata
low_findings.description: The documented audit event stores contact identifiers, content type, AI score, and token counts. This metadata is operationally useful but should be protected as customer engagement data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The audit fields are listed in the skill documentation. The risk is low because no credential or secret value is shown.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer Command Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby backtick execution locations correspond to Markdown fenced examples and plain workflow text. No executable Ruby, shell command invocation, or user-controlled command construction is present in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line-number review shows code fences and illustrative pseudo-operations, not runnable backtick execution. The skill contains only Markdown documentation.

dangerous_patterns.title: Outbound Request To Anthropic Messages API
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documents a POST request to https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages with a user-built prompt. This is expected for the feature but requires disclosure and data handling controls.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The hardcoded API endpoint and request body are shown directly in the skill. It is a transparent dependency rather than hidden behavior.

content.value_statement: Sales and marketing teams need timely follow-up content that reflects each contact's history. This skill turns contact context into draft emails, proposals, case study references, and review summaries.
actual_capabilities: Identifies lead and prospect contacts that are ready for follow-up.
Builds personalized prompt context from contact profile and interaction history.
Drafts follow-up emails, proposal emails, and case study references.
Stores generated content as drafts for human review and approval.
Logs content generation events for audit and performance tracking.
Summarizes generation results by content type and priority score.
limitations: Requires accurate contact data, interaction history, and AI scores.
Depends on external Claude API availability for AI-generated drafts.
Generated content still needs human review before sending.
Performance tracking depends on available email and conversion integrations.

use_cases.title: Revive warm leads
use_cases.description: Create concise follow-up drafts for contacts who have not received outreach after a recent positive interaction.

use_cases.title: Prepare proposal outreach
use_cases.description: Turn high-engagement prospect data into proposal emails with deliverables, expected outcomes, and next steps.

use_cases.title: Match case studies to prospects
use_cases.description: Generate case study references that connect a prospect's industry and pain points to similar customer results.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a simple follow-up
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a follow-up email for this lead using their name, company, last interaction, sentiment, and industry. Keep it under 150 words and include one clear call to action.

prompt_templates.title: Create a proposal email
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate prompt for a qualified prospect
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a proposal email for this prospect. Address their stated pain point, include three deliverables, mention expected measurable outcomes, and ask for a meeting.

prompt_templates.title: Reference a case study
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an email section that connects this prospect's industry to a relevant case study. Include the result, why it matters, and a short next step.

prompt_templates.title: Run a content batch
prompt_templates.prompt: Review eligible contacts by AI score, last interaction date, and prior proposals. Choose the best content type for each contact and provide a summary of drafts created.

output_examples.input: A technology CEO showed positive interest in Q4 marketing services and has not been contacted for seven days.
output_examples.output: A short follow-up email draft that references the Q4 discussion, mentions a relevant technology success story, and asks for a 15-minute strategy call.

output_examples.input: An eCommerce prospect has high engagement and wants to revamp their marketing strategy.
output_examples.output: A proposal email draft with tailored deliverables, expected revenue impact, a 60-day engagement suggestion, and a request to discuss details.

output_examples.input: A batch of eligible leads includes mixed AI scores and different industries.
output: A count of follow-up, proposal, and case study drafts created.
Priority groups based on AI score.
Sample draft labels for the contacts awaiting review.

best_practices: Review each draft for accuracy, consent, and brand voice before sending.
Limit prompt context to information needed for the specific content type.
Track approvals, sends, replies, and conversions to improve future prompts.
anti_patterns: Do not send generated emails without human review.
Do not include sensitive personal data that is not needed for personalization.
Do not rely on AI score alone when a contact has negative or outdated context.

faq.question: What content can this skill generate?
faq.answer: It can draft follow-up emails, proposal emails, case study references, and generation summary reports.

faq.question: Does it send emails automatically?
faq.answer: No. The skill stores drafts for human review and approval before any sending step.

faq.question: What data does it need?
faq.answer: It needs contact details, interaction history, engagement score, sentiment, and relevant case study context.

faq.question: Does it use an external service?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented workflow sends prompt content to the Anthropic messages API for generation.

faq.question: Is this suitable for regulated data?
faq.answer: Use caution. Minimize prompt data and confirm that external AI processing matches your privacy requirements.

faq.question: Can teams measure results?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow describes tracking opens, clicks, replies, conversions, and draft status when integrations are available.
